title: Active-window query ranking with CatBoost QuerySoftMax/QueryAUC
summary: CatBoost supports querywise objectives and QueryAUC over grouped items.
rationale: CatBoost supports querywise objectives and QueryAUC over grouped items. For this task, the natural query is all cars on the same race-lap; however, most laps have no realistic pit decision, so ranking signal is diluted. Restricting or upweighting groups near likely pit windows should sharpen the model around actual choice sets.
implementation_hint: Build groups as `(Year,Race,LapNumber)`. Train a ranker with `QuerySoftMax` or `YetiRankPairwise`, but only include full weight for rows with medium/high hazard proxies such as `TyreLife`, `DegPerTyreLap`, late-race dry compounds, or recent pace deterioration; downweight obvious non-window laps. Score with fold-local `QueryAUC:type=Classic` and blend by rank with the current binary models.
expected_effect: Could improve AUC by learning who is most likely to pit within each lap-level choice set, especially when only a few cars are plausible stoppers.
risk: If the weighting heuristic is wrong, the ranker can overfocus on a narrow regime and hurt global ordering.
